title: How to Wear Body Chains to a Festival
excerpt: Body chains aren't just for the beach. Here's how to style them for any festival without looking overdone.
content: Festival season means experimenting with things you'd never wear to work. Body chains fall into that category for most people — but they're way more wearable than you think. The key is knowing what actually works with the outfits you already own.

Start simple. If you've never worn a body chain before, don't go full harness on day one. A single belly body chain over a crop top or bikini is the perfect entry point. It catches light, adds movement, and you can take it off in thirty seconds if it doesn't feel right. You'll figure out your comfort level fast.

Layer them strategically. This is where body chains actually shine at festivals. Wear a dainty gold body chain under a sheer top, or stack two lighter chains at different heights. The layering creates depth and looks intentional rather than thrown-together. Just space them out — don't cluster everything at the same spot on your body.

Think about the actual activities. If you're pit diving at a music festival, a delicate chain might not survive. If you're walking around a craft market or watching sets from the side, you have more freedom. Our 316L stainless steel won't tarnish or break easily, but common sense still matters. A gold waist chain works better for lighter festival days than a complex body harness.

Match your vibe to your outfit, not the other way around. The best festival chains look like they belong with what you're wearing. Pair gold chains with warm earth tones or metallics. Silver or stainless chains work with cooler colors and grunge aesthetics. Don't force a chain onto an outfit just because you brought it.

Keep it secure. Body chains slip and shift when you're moving around. Wear yours over something fitted or tucked in, not over loose fabric that'll let it slide around all day. Tuck your shirt in if needed. A secure chain is a comfortable chain, and you'll actually enjoy wearing it instead of constantly adjusting.

Don't overthink the rest of your accessories. If you're already wearing a body chain, you probably don't need a statement necklace too. Skip the chunky jewelry and let the chain be the focal point. Simple rings, a basic watch, or minimal ear pieces work better. Your chain will get noticed without fighting for attention.

Remember: you can take it off anytime. This is the actual freedom of festival dressing. If halfway through the day a body chain feels like too much, remove it. There's no rule. Wear what makes you feel confident in that moment, and don't worry about looking like you planned it perfectly. Real style at festivals is about moving and enjoying yourself, not standing still for photos.

title: Body Jewelry for Brides and Bridesmaids
excerpt: Forget traditional wedding jewelry. Body chains are the move for couples who want to feel like themselves.
content: Your wedding day should feel like you. Not like you're playing a character in someone else's movie. If you've always loved body jewelry, why would you stop wearing it just because you're getting married? The answer is: you shouldn't.

Body chains have this quiet confidence that works perfectly for weddings. They sit on your skin, move with you, catch the light in unexpected ways. Unlike heavy necklaces that dig into your collarbones or rings that feel foreign on your fingers, body jewelry feels like an extension of you. It's there. It's beautiful. It doesn't demand attention.

For brides, there's something special about a delicate chain draped across your collarbone or shoulders under your dress. It's a secret detail only you and your partner will fully see. Or maybe you wear it over your dress entirely—a personal touch that says you're not following someone else's rulebook. The Bridal Forehead Chain and Bridal Hair Vine Chain are designed specifically for this moment, with the elegance of a bride who knows exactly what she wants.

Bridesmaids can play with this differently. Body jewelry lets you match the wedding aesthetic without losing your own style. A coordinated set of belly chains or back chains creates unity without looking like you've been dressed by someone else. Everyone feels good. Everyone looks intentional.

The practical side matters too. You're going to move, dance, maybe cry happy tears. You need jewelry that actually stays put. All our pieces are 316L stainless steel—waterproof, tarnish-resistant, built for a full day of living. You won't be thinking about your jewelry. You'll just be there.

If you're leaning toward something boho, the Boho Forehead Chain and Boho Hair Chain bring that effortless, romantic feel without trying too hard. They work whether you're getting married on a beach, in a garden, or anywhere else that feels like home.

Here's what El and Gio know from making these pieces by hand: the details matter because they're personal. Every chain is made in their Portuguese studio, no factories, no shortcuts. That care translates to jewelry that actually lasts and feels worth wearing again and again after the wedding.

Your wedding day isn't about fitting into someone else's idea of bridal. It's about showing up as yourself, feeling beautiful in that, and having pieces you'll actually want to wear for years. Body jewelry does that. It's bold and subtle at the same time. It's you.

title: How to Style Body Chains With Backless Dresses
excerpt: A backless dress is stunning on its own. Body chains make it unforgettable.
content: A backless dress shows off your back. So the real question isn't whether to add jewelry — it's what kind. You want something that complements the exposed skin, not competes with it. That's where body chains come in.

Back chains are made for this exact moment. They sit right where the dress ends, creating a visual line that draws the eye down your spine. The key is choosing one that matches the formality of your outfit. A delicate chain works for something simple and everyday. A bolder piece elevates a night out without trying too hard.

Think about the neckline of your dress too. If your backless dress has a high front, a shorter back chain keeps things balanced. If the front dips low, you can go longer in the back. It's about proportion, and your body is the canvas here.

Layering chains works if you're feeling it. A thinner back chain paired with a body chain or belly chain underneath creates depth. But don't overcomplicate it. Sometimes one piece says everything you need it to say. You'll feel the difference between wearing too much and wearing just right.

Material matters when something sits directly on your skin. Stainless steel doesn't tarnish, doesn't irritate, and doesn't break after one wear. You want something that moves with you and lasts through the entire night — and beyond. A good chain becomes part of your wardrobe, not something you worry about.

Color coordination is simpler than you think. If your dress is a dark color, warm metals like gold tones stand out beautifully. Silver works on everything, especially if your dress is metallic or neutral. The chain doesn't have to match your other accessories. It's its own statement.

Consider your hairstyle when planning your look. An updo or slicked-back hair gives the back chain room to shine. Loose waves create a softer frame. If you're wearing your hair down, a shorter chain keeps things from getting tangled. It's a small detail that makes a big difference in how the whole thing looks.

The best part about styling a backless dress with a body chain? You've made something beautiful even more interesting. You're not following a rule — you're trusting your instinct. And that confidence is what actually makes an outfit work.

title: How to Style a Back Chain (And Why It's the Most Underrated Piece You're Not Wearing)
excerpt: A back chain is one of those pieces that does everything without trying. Here's how to wear it, what to pair it with, and why it belongs in your rotation.
content: There's a reason back chains keep showing up everywhere — on the beach, at weddings, in editorials. They're the kind of jewelry that works hardest when you're barely thinking about it.

The concept is simple: a delicate chain worn around the back, visible when you turn around or wear anything open-backed. But the effect is anything but simple. It adds dimension, draws the eye, and makes even a plain black dress feel considered.

The easiest way to wear one is with a backless or low-back dress or top. The chain sits against your skin, catches the light, and creates a moment when you walk away. That's the whole move. Something like the Boho Back Chain is exactly the kind of piece that does that effortlessly.

For the beach, pair a gold back chain with a bikini. Let it drape naturally and forget about it — it won't tangle, it's waterproof, and it photographs beautifully against sun-warmed skin. The Pearl Backdrop Necklace is a favourite for this — the pearls catch sunlight in a way that feels almost accidental.

For a night out, a silver back chain under a strappy top gives that quiet-luxury feel without trying too hard. You don't need to stack it with anything. The Dainty Backdrop Back Necklace is the one for that — minimal, refined, and impossible to overdo.

Care tip: because back chains sit against your skin all day, wipe them down after wearing and store flat. 316L stainless steel handles everything — sweat, water, SPF — but a quick wipe keeps it looking brand new.

The gold and silver versions wear differently in personality. Gold feels warmer, more Mediterranean-summer. Silver reads cleaner, more modern. Both work. It really comes down to your existing jewelry and what metal you tend to reach for.
If you're new to body jewelry, a back chain is the perfect entry point. Low commitment, high impact. You can wear it daily or save it for occasions — either way it earns its place.

title: Waterproof Jewelry: What It Actually Means (And What to Watch Out For)
excerpt: Not all 'waterproof' jewelry is created equal. Here's what the label actually means, what to avoid, and how to make your pieces last.
content: Waterproof jewelry is one of those terms that gets thrown around a lot. But there's a real difference between jewelry that survives a splash and jewelry you can actually wear in the ocean every single day.

True waterproof jewelry is made from solid 316L stainless steel — surgical grade, the same material used in medical implants. It doesn't plate, it doesn't coat, and it doesn't tarnish because there's nothing to tarnish. The metal itself is the finish.

Gold-plated and gold-filled pieces are different. They have a layer of gold over a base metal. Water doesn't instantly ruin them, but over time — especially with chlorine, saltwater, and sweat — that layer wears thin and the base metal shows through.
So when you see 'waterproof' on a product, the first question is always: what's it made of? Solid stainless steel? You're fine. Plated? You'll want to be more careful.

At Bodystrands, everything we make is 316L stainless steel. We swim in our pieces, wear them to the beach, forget to take them off in the shower. That's the point — jewelry that lives your life with you. The Snake Belly Chain and the Cowrie Shell Anklet are two pieces people reach for exactly because of this.

The one thing even stainless steel doesn't love: harsh chemicals. Bleach, cleaning products, strong perfume directly on the metal. Spray your perfume first, let it dry, then put your jewelry on. That simple.

Storage matters too. Even waterproof jewelry benefits from being stored in a dry, airtight place when you're not wearing it. It keeps it looking new longer — not because it will tarnish, but because it prevents surface dust and micro-scratches.
The bottom line: if you want jewelry you don't have to think about, go solid stainless steel. It's not a compromise — it's actually the upgrade.
